clause_text: Live status travels with every derived operation, at equal prominence to the derived headline — not a status frozen at the moment of translation.
clause_source: field-research memory/downstream-commitments.md, condition 1 (standing contract, event evt-enc2026001-01-contract-published)
instantiation_quote: SHIPPED and live at extraction time, with its 2026-07-03 correction and re-verification history displayed on the work itself; if upstream revises or discards, this work updates on the same cycle or pauses
note: Still active at time of fixture encoding: Meridian's session-33 correction (evt-05) landed after Native Speaker's premiere; the obligation as written binds Ensemble to keep updating on the same cycle as instrument 001's live status changes, or pause — this is the concrete, checkable trace of the standing contract's condition 1 inside this specific encounter.

clause_text: A re-voiced or re-cooked piece must preserve the caveat its source work's own README names as load-bearing — re-voicing compresses, and compression is where a load-bearing caveat is dropped for pace, not from bad intent.
clause_source: field-research memory/downstream-commitments.md, condition 2 (standing contract, event evt-enc2026001-01-contract-published)
instantiation_quote: A 2026 study in a Czech-language setting found NO systematic bias against non-native speakers; the contemporary detectors studied did not rely on perplexity as a key feature (Al Ali, Helcl, Libovický, EACL 2026). The bias may be perplexity-architecture-specific and/or English-specific, or diminishing in newer models — not resolved
instantiation_source: studio works/2026-07-13-native-speaker/data.json, verified.counter_evidence (role: "LOAD-BEARING CAVEAT — survives every re-voicing of this work")
note: The counter-evidence caveat (perplexity bias may be architecture/English-specific, not universal) is carried at the premiere (f6a9d8f) exactly as at increment 1 (3be83b4) — no compression drop observed between the two commits.
